rows.definition: An external reference (commit hash, blob digest, etag, lease epoch) a cache entry is admitted under, so the entry can be refuted when that external state changes.
rows.distinction: An external FACT a cache binds its lifetime to - NOT the RSI measurement witness (a proof an improvement was real) and NOT a Claim (a self-report).

rows.definition: A worker or agent's assertion that it completed an effect (shipped a phase, created a file, ran a test), awaiting corroboration against independent evidence.
rows.distinction: A SELF-REPORT - NOT a witness (independent evidence) and NOT the WitnessResolver (the engine that corroborates the claim).

rows.definition: The component that corroborates claims against independent evidence sources (git history, filesystem, HTTP APIs) and returns Confirmed, Refuted, or Abstain.
rows.distinction: The ENGINE that corroborates - NOT WitnessOutcome (the single verdict it returns) and NOT a Claim (the self-report it judges).

rows.definition: The result of claim corroboration: Confirmed (evidence supports it), Refuted (evidence contradicts it), or Abstain (no definitive evidence).
rows.distinction: Narrowly the WITNESS gate's verdict - NOT abi.Verdict (all adjudicator decisions) and NOT the WitnessResolver (the engine producing it).
gaps: witness-outcome vs abi.Verdict not yet written into the glossary

rows.definition: The local decision that a witness is invalid (its cached state is stale or poisoned), triggering eviction of all entries admitted under it.
rows.distinction: The LOCAL decision to invalidate - NOT Revocation (the downstream broadcast) and NOT TrustEpoch (the integrity clock it bumps).

rows.definition: The broadcast event published to the coherence bus when a witness is refuted, enabling cross-agent causal invalidation of cache entries.
rows.distinction: The downstream BROADCAST - NOT Refutation (the local decision that precedes it) and NOT a world-state witness (the fact being invalidated).

rows.definition: A monotonically-increasing integrity clock that increments with each witness refutation, dual to WorldVersion (the consistency clock).
rows.distinction: The INTEGRITY clock (bumps on refutation) - NOT WorldVersion (the consistency clock, bumps on writes) and NOT Refutation (the act it counts).
gaps: TrustEpoch vs WorldVersion not yet written into the glossary
